name: Empanaditas (Pork Turnovers)
recipeIngredient: 2 oz Monterey Jack or Cheddar cheese, shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, cut margarine into flour until the mixture resembles cornmeal; sprinkle with ice water and mix with a fork to form soft dough.
2. Roll dough between 2 sheets of wax paper to form a rectangle about 1/4 inch thick. Remove paper and use a 2-inch diameter plain or fluted cookie cutter to cut dough into rounds. Continue cutting scraps until all dough is used (should yield 24 rounds).
3. Combine pork (or ham), cheese, mustard, and red pepper in a small bowl.
4. Preheat oven to 375°F.
5. Beat egg white lightly and brush an equal amount onto each pastry round. Spoon an equal amount of the meat mixture (about 1/2 rounded teaspoon) onto the center of each round; fold pastry over, turnover-fashion, to enclose filling. Press edges of dough together to seal.
6. Transfer turnovers to a nonstick cookie sheet. Add milk to egg yolk and beat lightly; brush an equal amount of mixture over each turnover and sprinkle with a dash of paprika.
7. Bake until turnovers are golden brown, 15 to 20 minutes. Transfer to warmed serving tray. Garnish with red pepper strips and cilantro.
Makes 8 servings of 3 turnovers each.
